content.title: ⚠️ Selling Qurbani animal fat?
content.subtitle: Find out what you must do if you have already sold it by mistake
content.body:
A few days after Eid-ul-Adha, it is a common sight in many areas for street vendors to go door-to-door to buy animal fat. Out of ignorance, regular people often sell the fat of their Qurbani animals to them. This action is not permissible. ❌ ❌ ❌

It must be kept in mind that selling the meat, fat, bones, hooves, or any part of a Qurbani animal is strictly forbidden. If anyone has sold it by mistake, the entire amount received must be given away in charity (Sadakah).

Under what condition is it permissible?

Selling any part of a Qurbani animal is not permissible unless there is an intention to donate the sale proceeds. However, if the intention is to donate the money, then it becomes permissible.

For example: We often sell the hides of Qurbani animals. The intention behind this sale is entirely to donate the full value to charity. Selling under this condition is permissible.

Therefore, if the fat is of no use to you and must be sold, it can be sold with the intention of donating the proceeds. Subsequently, the entire amount received from the sale must be given away in charity.

📚 References

  • Ila'us Sunan (17/259)

  • Bada'i as-Sana'i (4/225)

  • Fatawa Al-Alamgiri (5/301)

Share this post with the intention of spreading Islamic knowledge and help raise awareness among others.
